commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Mercy. Therefore he, the chief, and each of his adherents, can expect to find no mercy, James ii. 13. (Haydock)
By the most crying injustice, the Jews put to death, the man of sorrows, (Calmet) who had been pleased to assume our nature, and had not where to lay his head, Luke ix. 58. (Menochius)
